Module:
Research Methodologies  |
Semester:
3  |
Type:
UET3
VHS:
21h  |
Weekly hours:
1h30  |
Lectures:
1h30  |
TD:
-  |
TP:
-
Personal work VHS:
21h  |
Coefficient:
1  |
Credits:
2
Teaching Objectives
Teach students the skills and knowledge necessary to conduct effective scientific research
Cover key research steps: from topic formulation to final report writing, including literature review, research design, data collection and analysis, and oral presentation of results
Recommended Prerequisites
Scientific writing, language proficiency
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Introduction to Scientific Research
Definition and objectives of scientific research
Types of research (fundamental, applied, etc.)
Role of methodology in research
Importance of language proficiency
Research ethics in computer science: plagiarism, self-plagiarism, generative AI (ChatGPT, DeepSeek, etc.)
Research Topic Formulation
Identifying and selecting a relevant research topic
Literature review: searching relevant sources (articles, books), critical analysis of existing literature (state of the art), identifying gaps
Problem formulation: clear research question, research hypotheses
Research objectives: specific objectives, expected outcomes
Research Design
Choosing appropriate research methods (experimental, qualitative, quantitative, etc.)
Data collection planning (sampling, collection tools, etc.)
Research plan development
Data Collection
Implementing the research plan
Using data collection tools and techniques specific to computer science
Managing and organizing collected data
Data Analysis
Using data analysis software
Processing and analyzing collected data
Statistical methods and other appropriate analysis techniques
Interpreting results and discussing implications
Research Report Writing
Publication modes and structures: article, patent, thesis, book, poster, oral communication
Writing software: LaTeX, Overleaf, etc.
Structure and organization of a scientific research report
Clear and concise writing of report sections
Results presentation and conclusions discussion
Bibliographic reference management tools (APA, IEEE, Zotero, EndNote, etc.) and appendices
Oral Presentation of Results
Preparation and delivery of an oral research presentation
Scientific communication skills
Assessment
Semester exam (in-person): 100%
Bibliographic References
Michel Beaud (2020). L'art de la thèse. Éditions La Découverte.
Stefan Kottwitz (2021). LaTeX Beginner's Guide (2nd Edition). Packt Publishing.
Jean-Marie Dubois (2005). La rédaction scientifique: mémoires et thèses – formes régulières et par articles. Estem.
Michèle Lenoble-Pinson (1996). La rédaction scientifique: conception, rédaction, présentation. De Boeck Université.
Christine Gérard & Jean Germain (1985). Recherche bibliographique et documentaire: généralités. Faculté de Philosophie et Lettres.
